items.q: What makes the antigen-binding site of an antibody specific to one antigen?
items.correct: The variable region — its amino acid sequence (and thus shape) differs between antibodies, complementing a specific antigen.
items.misconception: All antibodies have the same binding site; specificity comes from where they go in the body.
items.random_wrong: Antibodies bind to any antigen they meet first.
items.why: Students miss the role of the variable region in determining specificity, thinking antibodies are interchangeable generic 'germ-killers'.

items.q: What are the three repeated stages of one PCR cycle?
items.correct: Denaturation (high temp, strands separate), annealing (lower temp, primers bind), extension (Taq polymerase synthesises new strands).
items.misconception: PCR has just one stage — heating the DNA until it copies itself.
items.random_wrong: PCR uses enzymes from bacteria to cut DNA into fragments, then sticks them back together.
items.why: Students don't grasp the three-temperature cycle; they think PCR is just 'heating DNA' rather than a precise three-step process repeated 30+ times.

items.q: What is the key difference between mitosis and meiosis?
items.correct: Mitosis produces two genetically identical diploid cells; meiosis produces four genetically different haploid cells (gametes).
items.misconception: Mitosis and meiosis both produce gametes; they are basically the same.
items.random_wrong: Mitosis happens in plants and meiosis happens in animals.
items.why: The similar names lead to confusion; students mix up which process produces gametes vs body cells.

items.q: In which phase does DNA replication occur?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: S. “G1” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In anaphase, what is pulled to the poles?
items.why: Correct answer: Sister chromatids. “Whole chromosomes” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A field of view contains 400 cells; 24 are in mitosis. What is the mitotic index?
items.why: The real figure is 0.06. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
